en_body:
"Trời ơi..!!" Only because of that cry do I love my own origin as a Vietnamese person! The ordinary country people of Việt-Nam: without lofty abstract knowledge: they do not live through a mirror of intellectual imagination or textbook language; they feel and live very truthfully, simply! When angered they may curse one another bitterly and wholeheartedly demolish every form of morality and ethics in language to "revenge" the wrong a friend or neighbor or stranger has done them. But in times "when the fire is dark and the lamps go out," in times "when weather turns against the body," whenever their village-neighbors need them, they leave a half-eaten bowl of rice, leave the sleep needed for a life laboring mind and body for each day's meal, to sincerely rescue, assist, and sustain one another.
 They may speak the same word "Chúa" with those who believe in GOD; they may light incense and eat vegetarian upon the Buddhist holy days, yet still offer Ông Địa a hand of bananas, asking to find something they have lost. But as in folk rhymes and ca-dao, they do not hesitate to mock and ridicule those holding office and power, even within religions, when those persons abuse their position.
 ...And whenever something is uneasy within them, they suddenly cry aloud, calling one sound, "Trời ơi..!!" which to me is so concise and endearing, containing a philosophy of Faith so free, simple, gentle, and immeasurable: for the meaning of "trời" carries no divisive religious metaphor, no intricate complexity of rules and doctrinal systems. It ought to be a symbol for the concept of something abstract, yet among all Vietnamese people when the cry "Trời ơi..!!" bursts forth, it is intimate, rustic, and to me alone wonderful, consummate, beautiful, miraculous, truly marvelous! Will you contemplate it?
 ...Trời ơi..!! How dearly I love the rice-field fragrance and country wind of Việt-Nam!

Trời Ơi...How Dear..!!
 "Trời ơi..!!" Only because of that cry do I love my own origin as a Vietnamese person! The ordinary country people of Việt-Nam: without lofty abstract knowledge: they do not live through a mirror of intellectual imagination or textbook language; they feel and live very truthfully, simply! When angered they may curse one another bitterly and wholeheartedly demolish every form of morality and ethics in language to "revenge" the wrong a friend or neighbor or stranger has done them. But in times "when the fire is dark and the lamps go out," in times "when weather turns against the body," whenever their village-neighbors need them, they leave a half-eaten bowl of rice, leave the sleep needed for a life laboring mind and body for each day's meal, to sincerely rescue, assist, and sustain one another.
 They may speak the same word "Chúa" with those who believe in GOD; they may light incense and eat vegetarian upon the Buddhist holy days, yet still offer Ông Địa a hand of bananas, asking to find something they have lost. But as in folk rhymes and ca-dao, they do not hesitate to mock and ridicule those holding office and power, even within religions, when those persons abuse their position.
 ...And whenever something is uneasy within them, they suddenly cry aloud, calling one sound, "Trời ơi..!!" which to me is so concise and endearing, containing a philosophy of Faith so free, simple, gentle, and immeasurable: for the meaning of "trời" carries no divisive religious metaphor, no intricate complexity of rules and doctrinal systems. It ought to be a symbol for the concept of something abstract, yet among all Vietnamese people when the cry "Trời ơi..!!" bursts forth, it is intimate, rustic, and to me alone wonderful, consummate, beautiful, miraculous, truly marvelous! Will you contemplate it?
 ...Trời ơi..!! How dearly I love the rice-field fragrance and country wind of Việt-Nam!
note: “Trời ơi” literally calls upon Heaven but functions as an intimate Vietnamese cry of wonder, distress, tenderness, or exasperation; Ông Địa is the household Earth God.
